channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Speaker Johnson moves on Senate's Trump budget bill as House GOP rebels threaten to defect
item.description: Speaker Mike Johnson is rallying House Republicans around the reconciliation process after the Senate passed its own version of such a bill.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump expected to host Netanyahu at White House next week
item.description: President Trump is expected to host Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu for a visit on Monday, a White House official told NewsNation.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Democrats propose bill to prevent Trump from relocating federal agencies outside DC
item.description: A cohort of Democratic representatives and senators are proposing legislation aimed at stalling President Donald Trump's efforts to relocate federal agencies outside of Washington, D.C., something the president has taken steps to start doing.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump Labor secretary hits the road as tariffs shock the markets
item.description: Many economists say the Trump administration’s global tariff strategy could spur a recession at home.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: Senate vote-a-rama on Trump budget bill kicks off
item.description: The Senate on Friday night kicked off its vote-a-rama, a process that is likely to conclude with Republicans adopting the budget resolution that will serve as a blueprint for enacting President Trump's domestic agenda.
item.dc:creator: Al Weaver, NewsNation

item.title: Justice Department seeks 7 year sentence for George Santos
item.description: Prosecutors said the length of the requested sentence — 87 months — reflects the serious nature of Santos’s “unparalleled” crimes.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: Congress has the power to halt Trump's tariffs. But Republicans aren't ready to use it
item.description: Republicans in Congress are watching with unease as stock markets tumble in the aftermath of President Donald Trump’s sweeping tariffs.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet its leaders following recent U.S. allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China hit brakes on TikTok deal after Trump announced wide-ranging tariffs, AP source says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump on Friday said he is signing an executive order to keep TikTok running in the U.S. for another 75 days to give his administration more time to broker a deal to bring the social media platform under American ownership.
item.dc:creator: FATIMA HUSSEIN, AAMER MADHANI and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate kicks off all-night 'vote-a-rama' as Democrats plan to press GOP on Trump, DOGE
item.description: Senate Republicans triggered a vote-a-rama on Friday in a bid to pass Trump's budget resolution after it ends.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Newsom pursues trade partnerships to shield California from tariff impact
item.description: Governor Gavin Newsom's office announced on Friday plans to shield California's economy from the ripple effects of the Trump administration's escalating tariff war.
item.dc:creator: Amber Coakley, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Interior secretary orders national parks to be open and accessible as workforce is cut
item.description: Under criticism for staff cuts across the country, Interior Secretary Doug Burgum is directing national parks to “remain open and accessible” and says officials will ensure proper staffing to do so.
item.dc:creator: MARTHA BELLISLE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: About 500 law firms sign brief challenging Trump's executive orders targeting the legal community
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump’s executive orders targeting the legal community pose “a grave threat to our system of constitutional governance and to the rule of law itself,” according to a court filing submitted Friday by more than 500 law firms.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Hegseth is to travel to Panama for meetings following Trump's demands for the canal
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth will travel to Panama next week to meet with leaders of the Central American country following recent Trump administration allegations of Chinese interference in the operations of the critical Panama Canal shipping lane.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Pro-Trump House candidate unleashes on 'partisan hack' Dem incumbent after launching rematch bid
item.description: Fox News Digital spoke to Ohio Republican Kevin Coughlin who is running to unseat Dem. Rep. Emilia Sykes in next year's midterm election in a closely watched race.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: FBI Director Patel leads charge to root out Chinese influence on US soil
item.description: With Republicans sounding the alarm over Chinese-owned farmland in the U.S., FBI Director Kash Patel has named eradicating CCP influence a top priority at the bureau.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Politics Friday: Budget begins to take shape as leaders huddle
item.description: MPR News politics editor Brian Bakst talks with the top budget adviser to the governor: Erin Campbell, commissioner of Minnesota Management and Budget. Then, a conversation with the new DFL Party Chair Richard Carlbom.
item.dc:creator: Brian Bakst and Matthew Alvarez, MPR News

item.title: Thune faces GOP divisions on critical budget resolution
item.description: Senate Majority Leader John Thune (R-S.D.) faces a number of divisions among Senate Republicans that could derail the Senate budget resolution, a measure that will be critical to passing President Trump’s legislative agenda later this year.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Senate GOP approves framework for Trump's tax breaks and spending cuts after late-night session
item.description: The Republican-controlled Senate has approved a budget framework that includes multitrillion-dollar tax breaks and spending cuts.
item.dc:creator: Kevin Freking And Lisa Mascaro, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: National Security Agency chief Timothy Haugh, deputy fired
item.description: Timothy Haugh, the head of the NSA, was among the top staffers fired from one of the nation's top intelligence agencies
item.dc:creator: Kevin Bohn, NewsNation

item.title: Fed chair: Tariffs could hike inflation, but more details needed
item.description: Powell's public appearance comes as Trump's "Liberation Day" tariffs continue to rock economic markets worldwide.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, NewsNation

item.title: The job market remains incredibly healthy -- but the tariff storm could upend things
item.description: Employers added 228,000 jobs in March, showcasing a solid labor market. But uncertainty over tariffs and tepid consumer spending may weigh on job growth in the months to come.
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley, MPR News

item.title: Florida lawmakers want to ease high school graduation requirements
item.description: State lawmakers are working to ease the anxiety of taking a test to help students earn their high school diploma. This includes math and English exams.
item.dc:creator: Alessandra Young, WFLA, NBC

item.title: States sue to block Trump’s election order, saying it violates the Constitution
item.description: Democratic officials in 19 states are suing over President Donald Trump’s attempt to reshape elections across the U.S., calling it an unconstitutional invasion of states’ clear authority to run their own elections. Minnesota Attorney General Keith Ellison is one of the 19 officials suing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: China imposes a 34% tariff on imports of all US products starting April 10
item.description: BEIJING (AP) — China announced Friday that it will impose a 34% tariff on imports of all U.S. products beginning April 10, part of a flurry of retaliatory measures following U.S. President Donald Trump’s “Liberation Day” slate of double-digit tariffs. The new tariff matches the rate of the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Michigan couple Paul, Christy Akeo freed from Mexico prison
item.description: Paul and Christy Akeo had been arrested in Cancún on March 4 after a high-end resort company accused them of fraud.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation

item.title: How the White House calculated reciprocal tariffs
item.description: President Trump triumphantly held up a poster board in the Rose Garden on Wednesday showing the reciprocal tariffs he plans to impose on nearly every country in the world.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, NewsNation

item.title: Here's Gov. Joe Lombardo's plan to solve the Nevada housing crisis
item.description: A $250 million plan to spur construction of homes and apartments for low- to middle-income Nevadans is projected to provide homes for 16,000 people, according to officials who presented it Wednesday night in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Pentagon watchdog to review Hegseth’s use of Signal app to convey plans for Houthi strike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Pentagon’s acting inspector general announced Thursday that he would review Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth’s use of the Signal messaging app to convey plans for a military strike against Houthi militants in Yemen.
item.dc:creator: TARA COPP, Associated Press, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump's reciprocal tariffs a 'double-edged sword': Sen. Ron Johnson
item.description: Trump promised the tariffs will bring about "the golden age of America," but economists have warned a recession could happen.
item.dc:creator: Jordan Perkins, NewsNation

item.title: After promising transparency, RFK guts public records teams at HHS
item.description: Despite promises for "radical transparency," Health and Human Services Secretary Robert F. Kennedy Jr. laid off many staff on teams that fulfill public records requests at health agencies.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin, MPR News

item.title: Unemployment extension for laid-off mine workers gets tangled up at Capitol
item.description: Two proposals include unemployment insurance benefit extensions. The Republican-sponsored one would tie the unemployment benefits to environmental regulation changes. A more-compact version has advanced, however.
item.dc:creator: Clay Masters, MPR News

item.title: Fired or frustrated federal workers may have eager employers waiting for them in Minnesota
item.description: For federal workers, the past few months have been filled with uncertainty as the Trump administration pushes ahead with efforts to reduce the size of government agencies. But whether they are forced or choose to leave, those workers may find employers eager to hire them.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats outnumber Republicans by just 2 voters amid statewide switch to nonpartisan
item.description: After Nevada Republicans surpassed their Democratic counterparts in voter registration for the first time in nearly 20 years, Democrats have once again taken over as the state's top party -- with a voter registration lead of two.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News
